entries.evidence: User request to bootstrap the quant-validation-agent harness with directory structure, policies, tools, middleware, tests, and docs.
entries.root_cause: No quantitative validation harness existed in the repository.
entries.targeted_fix: Create CLAUDE.md, README, harness policy files (system_prompt, data_contract, metric_policy, validation_workflow, threshold_policy, permission_policy, change_manifest schema/file), docs, skills, subagents, tools, middleware, tests, examples, memory and report folders.
entries.expected_benefit: Standardized, reproducible, observable quantitative validation for scoring / PD / LGD / EAD / scenario regression models.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive scaffolding under a new top-level directory; no operational system is touched.
entries.validation_method: pytest run on tools and middleware unit tests; manual review of policy and docs files.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the quant-validation-agent directory if scaffolding is rejected; downgrade unsuccessful changes by file-level reverts and append a rollback entry.

entries.evidence: Improvement #1 from completion summary — monitoring scenarios required a coded time × grade matrix.
entries.root_cause: skills/monitoring_validation.md described the procedure but no concrete tool was implemented.
entries.targeted_fix: Add functions build_time_grade_matrix, compute_period_psi_vs_baseline, summarize_default_rate_trend that work on row-level or pre-aggregated data.
entries.expected_benefit: Automates per-period × grade summaries needed for ongoing monitoring; reduces ad-hoc pandas code per validation.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive module; no existing function altered.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_monitoring_table.py covers row-level and aggregated paths plus baseline error handling.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete tools/monitoring_table.py and tests/test_monitoring_table.py.

entries.evidence: Improvement #2 — time-series regression of PD multipliers/scenario models needs autocorrelation and heteroskedasticity tests.
entries.root_cause: Initial diagnostics only covered VIF/condition index/JB; time-series concerns (autocorrelation, ARCH) were absent.
entries.targeted_fix: Add calculate_durbin_watson, calculate_breusch_godfrey, calculate_arch_test using statsmodels; manual fallback for DW.
entries.expected_benefit: Better detection of misspecification in scenario regressions where residuals are typically autocorrelated.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — new functions only; existing diagnostics untouched.
entries.validation_method: pytest cases against IID and AR(1) synthetic samples confirm DW < 1.5 and BG p-value < 0.05 on autocorrelated residuals.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the three new functions from regression_diagnostics.py and the corresponding tests.

entries.evidence: Improvement #3 — PD bias and similar metrics where both over- and under-prediction are bad require an absolute-value RAG mode.
entries.root_cause: assign_rag_status only supported higher_is_better and lower_is_better.
entries.targeted_fix: Add 'abs_lower_is_better' direction; reject negative thresholds for that direction; add tests covering both signs.
entries.expected_benefit: Correct RAG assignment for two-sided metrics like PD bias.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — existing two directions remain unchanged; new branch is opt-in.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_validation_summary.py covers all three directions and threshold validation errors.
entries.rollback_rule: Revert assign_rag_status to two-direction form and delete new tests.

entries.evidence: Improvement #4 — skills/challenger_model_validation.md described the comparison but had no automation.
entries.root_cause: Champion vs challenger comparisons were manual.
entries.targeted_fix: Add check_same_sample, compare_discrimination, compare_calibration with explicit same-sample assumption checks before metric deltas.
entries.expected_benefit: Reduces error-prone manual comparison code; enforces same-sample assumption.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive module.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_challenger_compare.py covers identical/different samples, discrimination delta, and calibration deltas.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete tools/challenger_compare.py and tests/test_challenger_compare.py.

entries.evidence: Improvement #5 — reproducibility benefits from a discoverable command-line entrypoint.
entries.root_cause: Validation runs were invoked ad-hoc via Python imports without a single entrypoint.
entries.targeted_fix: Create quant_validation_agent package with __main__/cli exposing 'run', 'thresholds', 'check' subcommands; subprocess-based pytest verifies behavior.
entries.expected_benefit: Consistent invocation surface; permission/PII guards usable as a CI gate via 'check'.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — CLI is new and does not modify tools/middleware logic.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_cli.py runs the CLI as a subprocess for run/thresholds/check.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the quant_validation_agent directory and tests/test_cli.py.

entries.evidence: Improvement #6 — markdown-only thresholds were not machine-readable and not auditable as code.
entries.root_cause: threshold_policy.md held reference numbers but they could not be looked up programmatically.
entries.targeted_fix: Externalize thresholds into harness/threshold_policy.json; add a read-only loader; document the JSON in threshold_policy.md; reference it from CLI.
entries.expected_benefit: Threshold changes become a single-file edit captured in change_manifest with human approval; loader prevents typos.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — values mirror the existing markdown reference; loader is read-only and never mutates the file.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_threshold_loader.py validates load, lookup, model-type listing, and missing-file error.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the JSON, loader, and tests; restore threshold_policy.md original wording.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #1 — calibration was descriptive only; needed inferential tests.
entries.root_cause: metric_calibration.py produced calibration tables and Brier but no formal goodness-of-fit tests.
entries.targeted_fix: Add Hosmer-Lemeshow chi-square test, Spiegelhalter Z test, and per-bucket binomial calibration test with explicit sample-size guards.
entries.expected_benefit: Stronger evidence base for PD calibration assessments; reduces over-reliance on bin-by-bin eyeballing.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive module, scipy already in dependencies.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests against well-calibrated and miscalibrated synthetic samples.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete tools/calibration_test.py and the test file.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #2 — KS plot coordinates and lift table were missing as ready-to-use functions.
entries.root_cause: build_decile_table existed but did not produce cumulative gain/lift or KS plot coordinates.
entries.targeted_fix: Add build_lift_table (cumulative gain, lift, base-rate normalized) and ks_plot_coordinates (rank_pct, cum_bad, cum_good, ks_distance).
entries.expected_benefit: Faster reporting of lift and KS visualization data; KS plot max distance equals calculate_ks output (verified).
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive module.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies first-bucket lift > 1, total cumulative shares = 1, and KS plot max == calculate_ks.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete tools/decile_lift.py and the test file.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #4 — different segments (retail, SME, LDP corporate) need different thresholds.
entries.root_cause: Original JSON had a single global metric block; segment-specific thresholds had to be reapplied manually.
entries.targeted_fix: Add by_segment.<seg>.metrics.<metric> overlay; loader.get_metric_threshold(segment=...) returns overlaid values, falls back to global, and reports the source.
entries.expected_benefit: Auditable, declarative segment-level RAG without code changes.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive structure; existing global-only callers behave identically because segment defaults to None.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies override values, fallback to global, and unknown-segment fallback.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove by_segment block from JSON and revert loader to the global-only signature.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #3 and #6 — single-command end-to-end execution and a recurring-finding writer were missing.
entries.root_cause: Validation runs and recurring-issue notes required custom Python scripts.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `validate` subcommand (scoring/PD: KS/AUROC/AR/PSI; LGD/EAD: MAE/RMSE/bias) with RAG via threshold_loader and segment overrides. Add `note add` subcommand that appends to memory/recurring_validation_findings.md and refuses guard violations (returns 5).
entries.expected_benefit: One-shot reproducible execution; recurring findings accumulate in a structured, guard-protected file.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Medium — new commands change CLI surface, but existing commands (run/thresholds/check) are unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest subprocess tests cover validate (scoring + lgd), missing-columns failure, segment override, note happy path, and note PII rejection.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove cmd_validate, cmd_note, and their parser entries; remove related tests.

entries.evidence: Follow-up improvement #5 — manifest entries should be schema-validated automatically.
entries.root_cause: Manifest entries were validated manually; future edits could violate the schema unnoticed.
entries.targeted_fix: Add jsonschema-based test that validates every entry, asserts unique change_ids, and constrains status values. Add jsonschema to project dependencies.
entries.expected_benefit: Continuous protection against malformed or duplicated manifest entries.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — adds a dependency, but jsonschema is widely available.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_change_manifest.py runs in the standard suite.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the test file and the jsonschema dependency.

entries.evidence: Improvement-related housekeeping — segment overlay should be documented near the markdown reference table.
entries.root_cause: threshold_policy.md mentioned the JSON file but not the by_segment overlay.
entries.targeted_fix: Append a short note pointing readers to the by_segment block in threshold_policy.json.
entries.expected_benefit: Easier discovery of segment-specific thresholds for human reviewers.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — documentation only.
entries.validation_method: Manual review.
entries.rollback_rule: Revert the appended paragraph.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #1 — scenario regression validation required wiring fitting + diagnostics + severity + floors into one orchestrator.
entries.root_cause: Existing tools handled each step but practitioners had to repeatedly assemble the pipeline.
entries.targeted_fix: Add fit_scenario_regression / predict_scenarios / check_scenario_severity / check_multiplier_floors / run_pipeline; expose `validate-scenario` in the CLI; add a small synthetic historical CSV.
entries.expected_benefit: One-shot scenario regression validation with all standard diagnostics; lower error rate vs hand-built pipelines.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Medium — orchestrator is new code, but underlying tools were already tested.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests for fit / predict / severity / floor / end-to-end pipeline; CLI subprocess test runs the example data with supplied multipliers.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the new module, tests, sample_macro_history.csv, and the validate-scenario subparser; remove cmd_validate_scenario from cli.py.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #2 — LGD RMSE/bias and EAD-ratio thresholds were missing, leaving CLI metrics Gray.
entries.root_cause: Initial JSON only defined mae_lgd; EAD has no portable absolute threshold across portfolios.
entries.targeted_fix: Add rmse_lgd, bias_lgd, mae_ead_ratio, rmse_ead_ratio, bias_ead_ratio thresholds. Update CLI validate to compute LGD MAE/RMSE/bias with thresholds and EAD errors normalized by mean realized (ratio metrics).
entries.expected_benefit: CLI now produces RAG for LGD and EAD, eliminating 'policy_undefined' Gray for these models.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — JSON additions and CLI branch changes; existing scoring/PD path unaffected.
entries.validation_method: Updated and new pytest cases assert RAG is in {Green, Yellow, Red} for LGD; EAD reports include mae_ratio/rmse_ratio/bias_ratio with RAG.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the new threshold entries and revert the EAD branch in cmd_validate to raw-Gray.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #3 — Hosmer-Lemeshow on sparse / LDP samples produced empty bins.
entries.root_cause: Quantile binning could yield buckets smaller than the χ² cell-size rule of thumb.
entries.targeted_fix: Add min_per_bin parameter performing greedy sort-and-pack so each bin has >= min_per_bin observations; tail merged into previous bin if under-filled. Reject min_per_bin < 1 and samples too small for two bins.
entries.expected_benefit: Stable HL test on small / sparse / LDP samples; explicit binning='greedy_min_per_bin' tag in output.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — quantile-binning behavior unchanged when min_per_bin is omitted.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers sparse-sample packing, too-small input rejection, and invalid min_per_bin.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore quantile-only signature.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #4 — CLI runs were not auto-recorded by run_logger.
entries.root_cause: Logging was available as a Python helper but not exposed at the CLI surface.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --log-dir to validate and validate-scenario; on success, write a structured run log via middleware.run_logger.write_run_log.
entries.expected_benefit: Each CLI execution produces a timestamped JSON record under logs/ for audit.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — only triggered when --log-dir is provided.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies a run_*.json file is written and contains request_summary / main_results.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove --log-dir parser entries and the run_logger calls.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #5 — lift tables needed a markdown formatter for reports.
entries.root_cause: Reports were produced manually from the DataFrame.
entries.targeted_fix: Add format_lift_markdown(lift_df, decimals=4); empty-DF placeholder; column-validation error.
entries.expected_benefit: Direct copy-paste of lift tables into validation reports.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive formatter.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy path, empty DF placeholder, missing-columns error.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the new function and tests.

entries.evidence: Round-three improvement #6 — operations team requested a future-ready monotonic-timestamp invariant for manifest entries.
entries.root_cause: Strict ordering had not yet been agreed upon.
entries.targeted_fix: Add an opt-in pytest test gated by environment variable QVA_STRICT_MANIFEST that asserts non-decreasing timestamps in append order. Default test run is unaffected.
entries.expected_benefit: When operations enables the env var, mis-ordered manifest entries fail CI immediately.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — test is skipped by default.
entries.validation_method: Manually exercised with QVA_STRICT_MANIFEST=1; default suite runs unchanged.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the opt-in test.

entries.evidence: Round-four improvement #1 — scenario regressions on macro series may have non-stationary inputs and lag-sensitive autocorrelation patterns.
entries.root_cause: Pipeline only ran lag-1 BG/ARCH and never tested unit roots; users could not control the lag from the CLI.
entries.targeted_fix: Add adf_stationarity_check / check_feature_stationarity helpers; expose run_stationarity_check, stationarity_alpha, autocorr_lags through fit_scenario_regression and run_pipeline; surface --autocorr-lags / --skip-stationarity / --stationarity-alpha on validate-scenario.
entries.expected_benefit: Practitioners can detect random-walk inputs and choose appropriate lag depth without editing code.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — defaults preserve previous behavior except for the additional stationarity output (additive).
entries.validation_method: pytest covers IID / random-walk ADF cases, opt-in/opt-out wiring, and short-series rejection.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove ADF helpers and revert run_pipeline / cmd_validate_scenario signatures.

entries.evidence: Round-four improvement #2 — EAD error normalizer was hard-coded to mean_realized; portfolios may require alternatives.
entries.root_cause: Policy did not declare which normalizer is canonical; CLI could not switch normalizers safely.
entries.targeted_fix: Add ead_metric_settings={normalizer, allowed_normalizers} to threshold_policy.json; add get_ead_metric_settings loader with consistency check; add --ead-normalizer to CLI validate (mean_realized | mean_predicted | total_exposure) with input validation.
entries.expected_benefit: Auditable EAD normalizer choice, with policy override and consistency enforcement.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — default normalizer remains mean_realized; existing CLI calls without --ead-normalizer behave identically.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies loader rejects inconsistent settings and CLI honors the override (mean_realized + total_exposure).
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the JSON block, the loader function, and the CLI flag.

entries.evidence: Round-four improvement #3 — markdown formatting was inconsistent across reports.
entries.root_cause: Each report builder formatted tables ad hoc; the existing format_lift_markdown was the only standardized renderer.
entries.targeted_fix: Create tools/markdown_renderers.py with render_dataframe_markdown / render_metrics_table / render_issue_table / render_regression_summary / render_calibration_table. Pure formatters with column-validation errors and empty-DF placeholders.
entries.expected_benefit: Reports use a single, testable formatting path; downstream rendering becomes uniform.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive module; existing format_lift_markdown unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy paths, missing-column rejection, and empty input placeholders.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the module and tests.

entries.evidence: Round-four improvement #4 — the standard 9-section markdown report had no automated builder.
entries.root_cause: Users had to compose section headers manually after each validate run.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `report` subcommand that consumes a validate JSON output and emits the 9 standard sections via markdown_renderers and validation_summary. Section 4 / 6 / 8 contain placeholders for human commentary; sections 1 / 2 / 3 / 5 / 7 / 9 are auto-filled from the JSON.
entries.expected_benefit: One-shot deterministic markdown reports for inclusion in validation packages.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — new subcommand only.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies all 9 section headers appear in the rendered output and missing inputs return exit 4.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove cmd_report and the report subparser.

entries.evidence: Round-four improvement #5 — operations needs a programmatic check that policy edits are tagged for human approval.
entries.root_cause: Threshold policy edits could land without a corresponding human_approval_required=true manifest entry.
entries.targeted_fix: Create middleware/policy_change_guard.py with compute_policy_digest, find_policy_change_entries, policy_governance_status, assert_policy_changes_approved (raises PermissionError on violations), and load_manifest helper.
entries.expected_benefit: CI-grade enforcement: any policy-touching manifest entry that lacks human approval fails the assertion.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — read-only helper.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers digest stability, substring matching, governance status on the real repo, and intentional violation cases.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the module and tests.

entries.evidence: Round-four improvement #6 — PD calibration validation required combining Brier, bias, Hosmer-Lemeshow, Spiegelhalter Z, and per-bucket binomial tests.
entries.root_cause: These were available individually but had to be wired together by hand for each PD validation.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `validate-pd-calibration` subcommand that accepts row-level or pre-aggregated CSVs. Auto-expands aggregated rows (count, defaults, predicted_pd) into per-observation rows for HL / Spiegelhalter / binomial. Applies threshold-based RAG for brier and pd_bias. Honors --segment, --hl-min-per-bin, --binomial-alpha, --log-dir.
entries.expected_benefit: One-shot reproducible PD calibration validation, including LDP-friendly bin packing and per-bucket inference.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Medium — new CLI surface; underlying tools were already tested.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers aggregated path against examples/sample_pd_timeseries.csv and missing-column failure.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove cmd_validate_pd_calibration, _expand_aggregated_pd, and the validate-pd-calibration subparser.

entries.evidence: Round-five improvement #1 — scenario regression results had no automated 9-section markdown report.
entries.root_cause: `report` only consumed validate JSON, ignoring validate-scenario output.
entries.targeted_fix: Accept --scenario-input on the report subcommand; add render_scenario_severity helper; render fit / ADF / severity / floor / time-series diagnostics into the standard 9 sections (with placeholders for human commentary).
entries.expected_benefit: Single command produces shareable scenario validation reports with deterministic structure.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — new branch only triggers when --scenario-input is provided.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies all 9 section headers and key blocks (시나리오 결과, Multiplier floor, Durbin-Watson) are present.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove --scenario-input branch in cmd_report and the render_scenario_severity helper.

entries.evidence: Round-five improvement #2 — manifest-only governance could not detect ad-hoc policy edits between approvals.
entries.root_cause: Without a digest baseline, policy drift was invisible to CI.
entries.targeted_fix: Add update_lock / load_lock / verify_against_lock / assert_policy_synced_with_lock. The lock pins the current digest with the approved change_id.
entries.expected_benefit: CI fails immediately when threshold_policy.json drifts from the last approved digest.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — new helpers; default behavior unchanged unless lock is consulted.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers round-trip update/verify, drift detection, missing-lock and drifted-policy assertion failures.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the lock helpers and tests.

entries.evidence: Round-five improvement #3 — policy edits could land with malformed structures (e.g., missing thresholds, unknown direction).
entries.root_cause: Loader only checked individual lookups, not the policy as a whole.
entries.targeted_fix: Add a JSONSchema for threshold_policy.json; expose validate_policy() in the loader that re-raises jsonschema errors as ValueError for uniform handling.
entries.expected_benefit: Structured policy validation (CI or pre-commit hook) catches direction typos, missing thresholds, and unknown top-level keys.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive validator; not invoked by default loader paths.
entries.validation_method: pytest validates the real repo policy and rejects deliberately malformed policies.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the schema file, validate_policy(), and tests.

entries.evidence: Round-five improvement #4 — scenario severity tables were rendered ad hoc.
entries.root_cause: Without a standardized renderer, severity output formatting drifted across reports.
entries.targeted_fix: Add render_scenario_severity(severity, multiplier_floors) that emits a pivot table (period × scenario), violation counts, and a floor table — all via render_dataframe_markdown for column validation and empty-DF placeholders.
entries.expected_benefit: Consistent severity-section formatting across CLI report and downstream tools.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive renderer.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies pivot rendering, violation counts string, and empty-pivot fallback.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the helper and its tests.

entries.evidence: Round-five improvement #5 — HL and Spiegelhalter tests produced p-values with no automated RAG, but practitioners still had to interpret them by hand.
entries.root_cause: The CLI deliberately reported only Brier and PD bias as RAG, leaving statistical tests as raw output.
entries.targeted_fix: Add hl_pvalue and spiegel_pvalue to threshold_policy.json (higher_is_better; G:0.05 / Y:0.01). Add --hl-rag opt-in flag to validate-pd-calibration that also emits RAG for these statistics; output records hl_rag_enabled=true and a sample-size sensitivity caveat.
entries.expected_benefit: Faster triage of PD calibration runs while preserving the explicit caveat about sample-size sensitivity.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — RAG only computed when --hl-rag is passed; thresholds added behind human approval.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies CLI output keys and RAG values appear when --hl-rag is set.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the two threshold blocks and the --hl-rag branch.

entries.evidence: Round-five improvement #6 — newcomers needed an end-to-end runnable tutorial covering all four model types and governance checks.
entries.root_cause: Existing example files were per-model and did not stitch together a complete CLI walkthrough.
entries.targeted_fix: Add examples/sample_validation_full_request.md describing the four CLI flows (scoring, PD calibration, LGD/EAD, scenario), the report subcommand, governance checks, and exit-code semantics. Includes a hard reminder that operational data must never be committed.
entries.expected_benefit: Reduces onboarding time and surfaces the governance steps that should accompany validation runs.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — documentation only.
entries.validation_method: Manual review.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the file.

entries.evidence: Round-six improvement #1 — RAG could be assigned from a malformed policy because validate* commands skipped schema validation.
entries.root_cause: Loader exposed validate_policy but no entrypoint invoked it.
entries.targeted_fix: Add _load_validated_policy(); call it from cmd_thresholds, cmd_validate (scoring/PD and LGD/EAD branches), and cmd_validate_pd_calibration. ValueError/FileNotFoundError surface as a structured 'policy_invalid' payload with exit code 6.
entries.expected_benefit: Schema-violating policies fail fast before any RAG is computed.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — successful loads are unaffected; failures previously raised raw exceptions.
entries.validation_method: pytest passes a deliberately malformed policy via 'thresholds --path' and asserts exit 6 + policy_invalid payload.
entries.rollback_rule: Inline-revert the helper and restore raw load_threshold_policy calls.

entries.evidence: Round-six improvement #2 — scenario report omitted RAG for fit metrics (R²/VIF/condition_index).
entries.root_cause: The renderer only printed raw values; policy thresholds existed for VIF/CI but were not consulted.
entries.targeted_fix: Add r_squared thresholds to the policy. Extend _render_scenario_report_markdown to compute R², max-VIF, and max-condition-index RAG when given a policy. Add --threshold-overrides to cmd_report so an alternative policy can be supplied (still schema-validated).
entries.expected_benefit: Auto-generated scenario markdown carries explicit fit-metric RAG; the overall section-1 RAG now downgrades on bad fit.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — overall-RAG logic falls back to the previous Yellow/Gray when no fit-RAG is computable.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies '적합도 RAG' block, r_squared/vif_max/condition_index_max keys, and --threshold-overrides exit 6 on malformed override.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the r_squared block, the fit_rag computation, and the --threshold-overrides flag.

entries.evidence: Round-six improvement #3 — policy governance was not callable as a single CLI step.
entries.root_cause: policy_change_guard helpers were only reachable from Python or test code.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `policy-governance` subcommand wrapping policy_change_guard. Reports manifest governance + lock digest comparison; exits 6 on missing approvals and 7 on lock drift when --require-lock is set.
entries.expected_benefit: Single CI command audits the policy approval chain.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — new command, no side effects.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers the green path (real repo), manifest violation (exit 6), and missing-lock with --require-lock (exit 7).
entries.rollback_rule: Remove cmd_policy_governance and the parser entry.

entries.evidence: Round-six improvement #4 — single-row aggregation pivots rendered with an anonymous index.
entries.root_cause: render_scenario_severity assumed a period × scenario layout.
entries.targeted_fix: Detect missing 'period' column and fall back to a 2-column scenario | mean_pred table.
entries.expected_benefit: Cleaner rendering when scenarios are aggregated across periods.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — period-bearing pivots take the original branch.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies the fallback table contains scenario / mean_pred columns and rows for base / adverse / severe.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore the previous always-period rendering.

entries.evidence: Round-six improvement #5 — manifest and lock could disagree silently.
entries.root_cause: No automated invariant tied the latest policy-change manifest entry to the lock file.
entries.targeted_fix: Add an opt-in test gated by QVA_STRICT_POLICY_LOCK: asserts the lock's approved_change_id matches the most recent policy-change entry and the live policy is digest-synced.
entries.expected_benefit: When operations enables the env var, manifest/lock divergence fails CI immediately.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — opt-in only.
entries.validation_method: Default suite: skipped. Manual run with QVA_STRICT_POLICY_LOCK=1 after lock update.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the opt-in test.

entries.evidence: Round-six improvement #6 — tutorial lacked a reference for thresholds/check/note/policy-governance flows.
entries.root_cause: Initial tutorial covered the four validate flows but not the auxiliary CLI surfaces.
entries.targeted_fix: Append Appendix A (thresholds, check, note add, policy-governance, report --scenario-input with overrides) and Appendix B (consolidated exit-code table).
entries.expected_benefit: End-to-end onboarding now covers governance and ad-hoc use, not just the four model flows.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — documentation only.
entries.validation_method: Manual review.
entries.rollback_rule: Trim the appendices.

entries.evidence: Round-seven improvement #1 — scoring/PD validate produced KS/AUROC/AR but no concentration metric with RAG.
entries.root_cause: decile_lift was available as a tool but not surfaced through validate.
entries.targeted_fix: Add lift_top_decile threshold (G:2.0/Y:1.5, higher_is_better) and --decile-rag opt-in flag to cmd_validate. When set, computes the top-decile lift via decile_lift.build_lift_table and emits RAG. Failures fall back to a Gray issue rather than a hard error.
entries.expected_benefit: Practitioners get concentration RAG alongside discrimination metrics in a single CLI call.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — RAG only computed when the opt-in flag is present.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies lift_top_decile appears in the metrics block with a valid RAG state.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the threshold entry, the --decile-rag branch, and the test.

entries.evidence: Round-seven improvement #2 — CI pipelines wanted compact JSON for jq parsing.
entries.root_cause: policy-governance always pretty-printed.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --json-only to cmd_policy_governance; emits single-line compact JSON via json.dumps separators.
entries.expected_benefit: Easier downstream JSON consumption in CI (single grep/jq line).
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — default formatting unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest asserts the stdout has no embedded newlines and parses cleanly.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the flag and conditional.

entries.evidence: Round-seven improvement #3 — scenario reports omitted any RAG signal for ADF stationarity.
entries.root_cause: Stationarity result was rendered as a table but never aggregated.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --include-stationarity-rag to cmd_report. Aggregate rule: target stationary => Green; any non-stationary feature => Yellow; non-stationary target => Red. Output includes the explicit caveat about ADF being a single unit-root test sensitive to sample size. Section-1 RAG now incorporates the stationarity state.
entries.expected_benefit: Faster triage of scenario regressions where input series may be non-stationary.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — block only appears when the opt-in is provided.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies 'Stationarity RAG' block and a valid RAG label appear in the markdown.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the stationarity_rag computation and the parser flag.

entries.evidence: Round-seven improvement #4 — operators had no CLI route to update threshold_policy.lock.json safely.
entries.root_cause: policy_change_guard.update_lock was Python-only; manual invocations were error-prone.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `policy-lock` subcommand: requires --change-id (CHG-####), defaults to dry-run with `would_write: true`, writes only when --confirm is supplied. Reports prior lock contents and the current digest.
entries.expected_benefit: Authorized actors can update the lock with a single audited command, with a built-in dry-run guard.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — write only when --confirm is explicit.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers dry-run (no file written), --confirm (file written with correct change_id), and invalid change_id (exit 4).
entries.rollback_rule: Remove cmd_policy_lock and the parser entry.

entries.evidence: Round-seven improvement #5 — long tables (e.g., many grades / periods) bloated reports.
entries.root_cause: render_dataframe_markdown emitted every row regardless of size.
entries.targeted_fix: Add max_rows to render_dataframe_markdown. Truncates to first N rows, appends an italic note '... K more rows truncated (max_rows=N)'. Rejects max_rows < 1.
entries.expected_benefit: Reports stay readable when models have many segments / time points.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — default behavior unchanged when max_rows is omitted.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies truncation note and absence-of-note when input fits within max_rows.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the max_rows parameter.

entries.evidence: Round-seven improvement #6 — CI usage required reproducible target invocation.
entries.root_cause: There was no canonical wrapper around the CLI surfaces.
entries.targeted_fix: Add a Makefile with help/test/test-strict/governance/smoke/ci/clean targets covering pytest, policy-governance --json-only, smoke runs over each example dataset, and report rendering. All targets run from project root and stay local-only.
entries.expected_benefit: Single command (`make ci`) drives standard CI, mirroring local dev steps.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — Makefile is additive.
entries.validation_method: make -n governance and make -n smoke produce expected commands without execution.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the Makefile.

entries.evidence: Round-eight improvement #1 — only render_dataframe_markdown supported max_rows; downstream renderers and the CLI report ignored it.
entries.root_cause: max_rows was added piecemeal in CHG-0042.
entries.targeted_fix: Propagate max_rows through render_metrics_table / render_issue_table / render_calibration_table / render_scenario_severity. Add cmd_report --max-rows (validates >=1, exit 4 on invalid).
entries.expected_benefit: Long tables (many grades / periods) stay readable in CLI-generated reports.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — defaults preserve previous behavior when max_rows is omitted.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers truncation behavior on every renderer and the CLI report flow.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the max_rows parameter from the helpers and the CLI flag.

entries.evidence: Round-eight improvement #2 — policy-lock could write a lock for a change_id that was never approved in the manifest.
entries.root_cause: policy-lock did not consult change_manifest.json.
entries.targeted_fix: Pre-check the manifest: the change_id must exist among policy-change entries (component contains threshold_policy.json) AND have human_approval_required=true. Failures exit 6. --skip-manifest-check escape hatch is documented as advanced/not recommended.
entries.expected_benefit: policy-lock and policy-governance enforce the same invariant; misconfigured lock writes are blocked.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Medium — existing callers must point --manifest-path at a valid manifest containing the change_id (or use --skip-manifest-check).
entries.validation_method: pytest covers approved happy path, --confirm write, invalid change_id format (exit 4), unknown change_id (exit 6), unapproved change (exit 6), non-policy component (exit 6), and --skip-manifest-check escape hatch.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the manifest pre-check block and the --manifest-path / --skip-manifest-check flags.

entries.evidence: Round-eight improvement #3 — validate-pd-calibration lacked a concentration RAG.
entries.root_cause: decile_lift was already wired into cmd_validate but not into cmd_validate_pd_calibration.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --decile-rag opt-in to validate-pd-calibration. Builds a top-decile lift table from pred_pd vs actual default_flag (PD acts as a risk score) and emits lift_top_decile RAG via threshold_loader. Failures append a Gray issue.
entries.expected_benefit: PD calibration runs now report concentration alongside Brier, bias, HL, Spiegelhalter, and binomial tests.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — opt-in only.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies lift_top_decile appears in the metrics block with a valid RAG state.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the --decile-rag branch and parser flag from cmd_validate_pd_calibration.

entries.evidence: Round-eight improvement #4 — `report-smoke` only rendered scoring + scenario; PD calibration / LGD / EAD smoke outputs were not converted to markdown.
entries.root_cause: Initial Makefile only covered the two flagship report flows.
entries.targeted_fix: Add public `report` alias depending on every smoke-* target. Renders all five smoke JSONs into _smoke_*.md files via cmd_report (scenario uses --include-stationarity-rag).
entries.expected_benefit: `make report` produces the full markdown set in one step; mirrors `make smoke` coverage.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — additive target; report-smoke retained for backward compatibility.
entries.validation_method: make -n report renders the expected command list.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the `report` target.

entries.evidence: Round-eight improvement #5 — LGD/EAD validate runs reported only aggregate MAE/RMSE/bias.
entries.root_cause: summarize_error_by_segment existed in tools/metric_lgd_ead but was not surfaced via the CLI.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --segment-detail and --segment-col to cmd_validate. When set, attaches report.segment_detail.{segment_col, rows} via summarize_error_by_segment. Missing column emits a Gray issue rather than failing.
entries.expected_benefit: Practitioners can read per-segment error breakdowns directly from the validate JSON.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — opt-in; defaults unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy path against examples/sample_lgd_ead_data.csv (collateral_type) and missing-column gray issue.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the segment_detail block and the two parser flags.

entries.evidence: Round-eight improvement #6 — tutorial did not cover the Makefile-driven workflow.
entries.root_cause: Makefile was added in CHG-0043 but never documented in the onboarding tutorial.
entries.targeted_fix: Append Appendix C describing make help/test/test-strict/governance/smoke/report/ci/clean and the recommended CI flow including policy-governance --require-lock and policy-lock dry-run/--confirm.
entries.expected_benefit: Newcomers can drive the entire validation suite without reading individual CLI command reference.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — documentation only.
entries.validation_method: Manual review.
entries.rollback_rule: Trim Appendix C.

entries.evidence: Round-nine improvement #1 — validate-scenario predictions list bloated reports for long horizons.
entries.root_cause: Predictions list was never truncated.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --max-predictions to validate-scenario. Truncates the predictions list, records predictions_total and predictions_truncated. Rejects values <1 with exit 4.
entries.expected_benefit: Compact JSON for long-horizon scenarios; full counts still recoverable from metadata.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — defaults preserve previous behavior when the flag is omitted.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers truncation + invalid value branches end-to-end.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the --max-predictions branch and parser flag.

entries.evidence: Round-nine improvement #2 — triage required scanning every metric block to derive an overall RAG.
entries.root_cause: cmd_validate emitted per-metric RAG only.
entries.targeted_fix: Aggregate worst RAG across all metric blocks into report.overall_rag (Red > Yellow > Green > Gray; Gray when no metrics or any Gray with no Red/Yellow).
entries.expected_benefit: Single field for triage in CI / dashboards.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive field; existing fields unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies overall_rag is one of {Green, Yellow, Red, Gray} after a real validate run.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the overall_rag aggregate block.

entries.evidence: Round-nine improvement #3 — analysts could accidentally load PII-bearing CSVs without a guard.
entries.root_cause: read_csv_safely never inspected payload contents.
entries.targeted_fix: Add scan_pii (and pii_max_rows) to read_csv_safely. Detected PII raises PermissionError with type list and refuses to return the dataframe. Local import keeps middleware off the default code path.
entries.expected_benefit: Default path unchanged; opt-in scan blocks accidental PII ingestion at the boundary.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — opt-in only; default callers see no behavior change.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy path, scan_pii blocking email + RRN, scan disabled, clean dataset passing.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop scan_pii / pii_max_rows parameters and the import block.

entries.evidence: Round-nine improvement #4 — operations wanted a soft-fail signal between exit 0 and exit 7 for lock drift.
entries.root_cause: policy-governance only had hard --require-lock (exit 7).
entries.targeted_fix: Add --exit-on-yellow to policy-governance. Returns exit 1 when manifest governance is fine but the lock is missing or drifted. Documented in the docstring.
entries.expected_benefit: Three-tier CI gate: 0 ok / 1 soft-warn / 7 hard-fail.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — soft-warn only triggers under the new opt-in.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers exit 1 with missing lock and exit 0 with synthetic in-sync lock for the real repo policy.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the --exit-on-yellow flag and conditional.

entries.evidence: Round-nine improvement #5 — reviewing macro scenarios in isolation required running every smoke target first.
entries.root_cause: No targeted Make rule for the stationarity-RAG report.
entries.targeted_fix: Add stationarity-report target depending on smoke-scenario only. Renders reports/_smoke_scenario_stationarity.md with --include-stationarity-rag.
entries.expected_benefit: Single-step inspection of macro scenario stationarity.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — additive target.
entries.validation_method: make -n stationarity-report renders the expected commands without execution.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the new target.

entries.evidence: Round-nine improvement #6 — manifest could land trivially short or sparse entries unnoticed.
entries.root_cause: Existing manifest tests covered schema + uniqueness only.
entries.targeted_fix: Add three sanity tests: component must be a non-trivial string without control chars; evidence/targeted_fix/rollback_rule/validation_method must each be at least 8 chars; CHG-#### sequence must be dense (no gaps).
entries.expected_benefit: Catches sloppy manifest entries before commit; preserves audit value.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — current manifest already passes the new checks.
entries.validation_method: pytest runs the new sanity tests against the live manifest.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the three new tests.

entries.evidence: Round-ten improvement #1 — validate-scenario lacked an aggregate RAG; consumers had to inspect severity and floors separately.
entries.root_cause: validate-scenario emitted severity / multiplier_floors but no overall_rag.
entries.targeted_fix: Compute overall_rag (Red on severity violation or floor violation, Yellow otherwise) right after run_pipeline. Documented as Yellow until report attaches fit_rag.
entries.expected_benefit: Single-field triage from the validate-scenario output; consistent with cmd_validate.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive field; existing fields unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies overall_rag == 'Yellow' on the synthetic happy path.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the overall_rag computation block.

entries.evidence: Round-ten improvement #2 — multiple validate runs needed a single CLI to aggregate RAGs.
entries.root_cause: There was no way to combine outputs without writing custom Python.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `summary` subcommand: takes repeated --input paths, classifies each as scenario/validate/missing/invalid_json, derives overall_rag (uses inputs' field when present, else recomputes), reports worst_rag. --fail-on-red exits 6; --json-only emits compact JSON.
entries.expected_benefit: One-command CI roll-up of all model validations.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — read-only aggregator.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy aggregate path, missing/invalid file resilience, and --fail-on-red.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove cmd_summary and the parser block.

entries.evidence: Round-ten improvement #3 — risky-command pattern set did not cover modern infra teardown commands.
entries.root_cause: Original list focused on git/docker/kubectl apply.
entries.targeted_fix: Extend DEFAULT_RISK_PATTERNS with kubectl delete/exec, helm install/upgrade/uninstall, terraform destroy, aws s3 rm, gcloud sql instances delete.
entries.expected_benefit: Wider catch surface for accidental destructive infra commands.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive patterns; previously-clean inputs remain clean unless they actually use the new keywords.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies the new patterns each match a representative command.
entries.rollback_rule: Trim DEFAULT_RISK_PATTERNS back to the previous list.

entries.evidence: Round-ten improvement #4 — accumulating PD calibration runs required manual file naming.
entries.root_cause: validate-pd-calibration had only --out (single fixed path).
entries.targeted_fix: Add --out-pattern: accepts a path with the literal token {ts}; substitutes the current YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_ffffff. report.resolved_out_path captures the actual file written.
entries.expected_benefit: Multi-run accumulation without manual filename collisions; log artifact path stays accurate.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — only triggered when --out is omitted and --out-pattern is provided.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies the resolved path replaces {ts} and the file is created.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop --out-pattern, restore args.out usage in run_logger artifacts.

entries.evidence: Round-ten improvement #5 — degenerate inputs could yield NaN/inf PSI silently.
entries.root_cause: calculate_psi / calculate_psi_by_bucket only validated inputs, not the result.
entries.targeted_fix: After computing PSI, check np.isfinite(psi); raise ValueError with a diagnostic message when the result is non-finite.
entries.expected_benefit: Stops downstream RAG / report generation on degenerate distributions instead of silently emitting NaN.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — well-formed inputs continue to return numeric PSI.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies constant-value distributions raise ValueError.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the np.isfinite guards.

entries.evidence: Round-ten improvement #6 — markdown threshold table could drift away from the JSON without warning.
entries.root_cause: No automated parity check between threshold_policy.md and threshold_policy.json.
entries.targeted_fix: Add tests/test_policy_consistency.py: tracks KS / AUROC / AR / PSI / Brier / VIF rows in the markdown and asserts the JSON-defined green/yellow thresholds appear there. Also asserts every by_segment key is mentioned in the markdown.
entries.expected_benefit: Markdown stays in lock-step with the machine-readable policy.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — current files already pass.
entries.validation_method: pytest runs the new module.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the test module.

entries.evidence: Round-eleven improvement #1 — validate-scenario needed the same timestamped accumulator option as validate-pd-calibration.
entries.root_cause: Only validate-pd-calibration had --out-pattern.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --out-pattern to validate-scenario. Substitutes {ts} with the current YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_ffffff and records the resolved path under resolved_out_path. Carry-forward into run_logger artifacts.
entries.expected_benefit: Multi-run accumulation of scenario regressions without filename collisions.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — only triggered when --out is omitted.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies resolved path substitution and file existence.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop --out-pattern handling block and parser flag.

entries.evidence: Round-eleven improvement #2 — overall_rag aggregation was added in round 9 but not directly tested for LGD/EAD branches.
entries.root_cause: Test coverage was scoping-limited to scoring/PD.
entries.targeted_fix: Add explicit overall_rag tests for cmd_validate LGD and EAD paths against the example dataset.
entries.expected_benefit: Regression-detect future changes to the LGD/EAD branches that might skip overall_rag emission.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — test additions only.
entries.validation_method: pytest asserts overall_rag presence and valid state.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the two tests.

entries.evidence: Round-eleven improvement #3 — binomial per-bucket results required manual counting to know how many buckets rejected H0.
entries.root_cause: Only the raw per-bucket records were emitted.
entries.targeted_fix: Compute binomial_summary = {n_buckets, n_buckets_rejecting_h0, alpha} after the per-bucket calibration test, append to the PD calibration report.
entries.expected_benefit: Single number for triage without re-scanning records.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive field.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies summary keys and the bounds 0 <= rejecting <= n_buckets.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the summary computation block.

entries.evidence: Round-eleven improvement #4 — CLI version was not surfaced; operators could not confirm which build they had.
entries.root_cause: No --version flag on the top-level parser.
entries.targeted_fix: Add a global --version flag printing `quant_validation_agent <__version__>` and exiting cleanly. __version__ imported from the package __init__.
entries.expected_benefit: Quick provenance check from the CLI.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — flag only.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies --version exits 0 with the expected prefix.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove the --version add_argument call.

entries.evidence: Round-eleven improvement #5 — CI gate required a single recipe that fails on any Red across all smoke outputs.
entries.root_cause: Existing ci/smoke targets did not aggregate via the summary subcommand.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `verify` target depending on test + governance + smoke; pipes every smoke JSON through `summary --fail-on-red` and prints a success banner.
entries.expected_benefit: Single command (`make verify`) enforces the full quality gate.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — additive target.
entries.validation_method: make -n verify shows the expected command list.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the verify target.

entries.evidence: Round-eleven improvement #6 — CLI surface area kept growing without a single source-of-truth reference.
entries.root_cause: No automation captured argparse --help output to disk.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `docs-cli` subcommand: builds the parser, captures top-level + every subcommand --help, writes docs/cli_reference.md (override path via --out). Header notes it is auto-generated.
entries.expected_benefit: Reviewers can browse the full CLI surface without running every subcommand; reference stays consistent with code.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — read-only writer; runs in milliseconds.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies docs/cli_reference.md exists and references every key subcommand.
entries.rollback_rule: Remove cmd_docs_cli and the parser entry.

entries.evidence: Round-twelve improvement #1 — docs-cli was available but no committed baseline / drift test existed.
entries.root_cause: The reference was generated on demand and not enforced in CI.
entries.targeted_fix: Commit a baseline docs/cli_reference.md plus a pytest module that re-runs docs-cli into a tmp_path and diffs against the committed file. Stale references fail the test with a clear remediation hint.
entries.expected_benefit: CLI documentation stays in lock-step with code; reviewers see a single authoritative reference.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — drift triggers a failing test, not silent skew.
entries.validation_method: pytest re-generates and compares against the committed baseline.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete docs/cli_reference.md and the test module.

entries.evidence: Round-twelve improvement #2 — validate-pd-calibration assumed pred_pd was a risk score without exposing the inference.
entries.root_cause: decile-rag used higher_is_worse=True implicitly with no cross-check.
entries.targeted_fix: When --decile-rag is set, call target_validation.infer_score_direction and record the result under report.score_direction so reviewers can spot inverted PDs.
entries.expected_benefit: Direction inference becomes part of the audit trail; flips on real data surface immediately.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — opt-in feature path only.
entries.validation_method: pytest asserts score_direction is present and higher_is_worse on the synthetic dataset.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the infer_score_direction call and report.score_direction field.

entries.evidence: Round-twelve improvement #3 — operators wanted version + runtime metadata in JSON form, not just the argparse banner.
entries.root_cause: Only --version was available, which prints a single string.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `version` subcommand emitting {package, version, python, platform}. --json-only compresses to a single line for jq pipelines.
entries.expected_benefit: CI logs include structured version metadata; argparse --version remains for humans.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive subcommand.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers default and --json-only output shapes.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop cmd_version and the parser entry.

entries.evidence: Round-twelve improvement #4 — inverted green/yellow thresholds silently corrupted RAG output.
entries.root_cause: assign_rag_status compared values but never sanity-checked the threshold pair direction.
entries.targeted_fix: Add direction-aware sanity: higher_is_better requires green > yellow; lower_is_better and abs_lower_is_better require green < yellow. Violations raise ValueError before any classification.
entries.expected_benefit: Misconfigured policy or override fails immediately instead of producing nonsensical RAG.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — current threshold_policy.json already satisfies the invariant.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy path plus three new direction-specific inversion tests.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the new sanity-check block.

entries.evidence: Round-twelve improvement #5 — PD calibration validation lacked a focused request template.
entries.root_cause: examples/sample_validation_request.md focused on scoring; PD users had to adapt it.
entries.targeted_fix: Add a request template covering data path, column mapping, indicator selection (Brier / bias / HL / Spiegelhalter / binomial / opt-in --hl-rag, --decile-rag), execution snippet, threshold policy reference, success criteria, and known limits.
entries.expected_benefit: Faster onboarding for PD calibration validation runs.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — documentation only.
entries.validation_method: Manual review.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the file.

entries.evidence: Round-twelve improvement #6 — run-log filenames could collide under concurrent CLI invocations within the same microsecond.
entries.root_cause: Filename was timestamp-only (YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_ffffff).
entries.targeted_fix: Append process PID and a monotonically incrementing 6-digit counter to the filename. A burst of 50 writes in a tight loop now yields 50 distinct paths.
entries.expected_benefit: Concurrent or rapid sequential writes no longer overwrite each other.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — filename pattern is additive; existing parsers still glob run_*.json.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies uniqueness across a 50-element burst and that the filename includes the PID segment.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore the original timestamp-only filename builder.

entries.evidence: Round-thirteen improvement #1 — only `report --scenario-input` could emit stationarity RAG; reviewers wanted it inline on validate-scenario.
entries.root_cause: Stationarity-RAG aggregation lived only in the markdown renderer path.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --include-stationarity-rag to validate-scenario. Aggregates ADF results into stationarity_rag (target stationary => Green; any non-stationary feature => Yellow; non-stationary target => Red) with a sample-size caveat, and folds the state into overall_rag.
entries.expected_benefit: Single CLI invocation surfaces stationarity status; downstream tooling can read JSON without re-running the report renderer.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — opt-in flag; default output unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies stationarity_rag appears with a valid RAG state.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the new block and parser flag.

entries.evidence: Round-thirteen improvement #2 — write_dataframe_safely silently produced non-.csv files when the caller mistyped the path.
entries.root_cause: No path-suffix sanity check.
entries.targeted_fix: Emit a warnings.warn() when the destination does not end with .csv. File is still written so the caller can downgrade or escalate; tests cover warn-on-mismatch and silent-on-match.
entries.expected_benefit: Mistyped output paths surface immediately during dev/CI.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — warning only.
entries.validation_method: pytest uses warnings.catch_warnings to assert the warning fires only on non-.csv targets.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the warnings.warn() block.

entries.evidence: Round-thirteen improvement #3 — name-only leakage detection missed datetime/flag columns whose names were short or domain-specific.
entries.root_cause: detect_leakage_candidates only checked column names against patterns.
entries.targeted_fix: Add detect_leakage_candidates_df: combines name heuristics with dtype heuristics. Datetime columns ending in _date/_at/_time are flagged; integer/bool columns ending in _flag/_observed/_realized are flagged. Each match records reasons (name / dtype:datetime / dtype:flag).
entries.expected_benefit: Catches realized timestamp / flag columns that slip past the name regex alone.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive function; original detect_leakage_candidates unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest builds a synthetic DataFrame and asserts the expected reasons appear for default_date / default_flag / neutral_flag while leaving x1 clean.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the new function and test.

entries.evidence: Round-thirteen improvement #4 — `summary` was print-only; CI flows could not capture its JSON.
entries.root_cause: No --out option on the summary subcommand.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --out to cmd_summary; writes the same JSON the subcommand prints, after creating parent directories.
entries.expected_benefit: Pipelines can persist the aggregate without redirecting stdout.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — opt-in path.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies file content matches the stdout structure.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the --out branch and parser flag.

entries.evidence: Round-thirteen improvement #5 — `make clean` removed reports but also wiped __pycache__, making fresh runs slow.
entries.root_cause: clean was a single recipe.
entries.targeted_fix: Split into clean-reports (just the smoke artifacts) and clean (clean-reports + __pycache__). Both targets explicitly preserve docs/cli_reference.md.
entries.expected_benefit: Selective cleaning; faster CI cold starts when only stale reports need removal.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — additive target.
entries.validation_method: make -n clean-reports / clean produce the expected command list.
entries.rollback_rule: Re-merge the two recipes.

entries.evidence: Round-thirteen improvement #6 — exit-code table omitted code 1 (the --exit-on-yellow soft warn) and the summary --fail-on-red contribution to code 6.
entries.root_cause: Appendix B was authored before those flags landed.
entries.targeted_fix: Add row for exit 1 (policy-governance --exit-on-yellow) and clarify that summary --fail-on-red also produces code 6.
entries.expected_benefit: Onboarding tutorial now matches the actual CLI semantics.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — documentation only.
entries.validation_method: Manual review.
entries.rollback_rule: Trim the new row / clarification.

entries.evidence: Round-fourteen improvement #1 — malformed PD inputs (>1.0 or negative) were silently averaged.
entries.root_cause: calculate_pd_bias only checked for non-null rows.
entries.targeted_fix: Add explicit [0,1] range checks on pred_pd_col and default_col; raise ValueError with the offending column name.
entries.expected_benefit: Invalid PD or default rates fail loudly before reaching the calibration table.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — current tests already use [0,1] values.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers pred>1 and negative default rate cases.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the range-check block.

entries.evidence: Round-fourteen improvement #2 — model_types accepted any free-form string; typos like 'scoring2' would pass schema validation.
entries.root_cause: metricDefinition.model_types items were typed as plain string.
entries.targeted_fix: Add a $defs/modelType enum covering scoring/pd/lgd/ead/regression/pd_multiplier/monitoring. metricDefinition.model_types now uses the enum.
entries.expected_benefit: Typos in model_types fail schema validation immediately.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — current policy.json values already match the enum.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies the policy still validates and that an unknown model_type is rejected.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore plain-string item type.

entries.evidence: Round-fourteen improvement #3 — reviewers wanted a single-command path to see the 9-section markdown alongside the validate JSON.
entries.root_cause: Today this required running validate then report sequentially.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --explain to cmd_validate. After printing JSON, also prints a `--- markdown ---` divider followed by _render_report_markdown(report).
entries.expected_benefit: One-shot human-readable view; report subcommand still preferred for committed artifacts.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — opt-in path.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies both the divider and the standard markdown header appear in stdout.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the --explain branch and parser flag.

entries.evidence: Round-fourteen improvement #4 — pandas nullable dtypes (Int64/Float64 with pd.NA) caused TypeError in detect_grade_inversion.
entries.root_cause: float() on pd.NA raises; dropna() did not always remove pd.NA from nullable columns.
entries.targeted_fix: Convert the metric column to float64 via pd.to_numeric(..., errors='coerce') and drop NaN rows in both grade and metric columns before sorting.
entries.expected_benefit: Nullable-dtype inputs from modern pandas pipelines work transparently.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — float64 conversion is a no-op for plain floats.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers normal, violation, and pd.NA scenarios across Float64 / Int64 columns.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore the original dropna()-only implementation.

entries.evidence: Round-fourteen improvement #5 — PID+counter filename change had no multiprocess test.
entries.root_cause: Round 12 only added a single-process burst test.
entries.targeted_fix: Add concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor-based test that spawns three workers writing twenty records each (60 total) and asserts every filename is unique.
entries.expected_benefit: Multi-process collisions remain impossible as the codebase evolves.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure test; uses module-level _worker for spawn safety.
entries.validation_method: pytest spawns subprocesses via ProcessPoolExecutor and inspects the union of returned paths.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the new test.

entries.evidence: Round-fourteen improvement #6 — no synthetic dataset exercised the scenario severity violation branch end-to-end.
entries.root_cause: Tests had only the well-ordered macro sample.
entries.targeted_fix: Add examples/sample_scenario_failure_case.csv with 2026Q1 severe < adverse. Add a CLI test that runs validate-scenario over it and asserts n_violation_total >= 1 and overall_rag == 'Red'.
entries.expected_benefit: Regression-detects future changes that accidentally hide severity violations.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — synthetic data and a new test only.
entries.validation_method: pytest end-to-end through validate-scenario.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the CSV and the new test.

entries.evidence: Round-fifteen improvement #1 — validate-pd-calibration emitted aggregate metrics only; reviewers wanted per-bucket calibration detail.
entries.root_cause: Only validate (LGD/EAD) had --segment-detail.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --segment-detail to validate-pd-calibration. When set with --bucket-col, attaches report.segment_detail.{bucket_col, rows} via metric_calibration.build_calibration_table. Missing --bucket-col emits a Gray issue rather than failing.
entries.expected_benefit: Symmetric --segment-detail surface across LGD/EAD and PD calibration runs.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — opt-in only.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy path and missing-bucket Gray-issue path.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the segment_detail block in cmd_validate_pd_calibration and the parser flag.

entries.evidence: Round-fifteen improvement #2 — comparing validate runs over time required custom code.
entries.root_cause: No diff helper for validate JSON outputs.
entries.targeted_fix: Add tools/diff_reports.py with diff_metric_blocks (per-metric delta + RAG transition) and diff_overall_rag (overall regression flag). Pure-function module; no I/O.
entries.expected_benefit: Foundation for the `compare` CLI subcommand.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive module.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers metric delta computation, missing-side handling, and regression detection.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the module and its tests.

entries.evidence: Round-fifteen improvement #3 — operators needed a CI-friendly command to flag RAG regressions between two runs.
entries.root_cause: diff_reports lived only in Python; no CLI surface.
entries.targeted_fix: Add `compare` subcommand wrapping diff_reports. --base and --current required, --out persists JSON, --json-only compresses, --fail-on-regression exits 6 when overall_rag worsens.
entries.expected_benefit: Single command for week-over-week / month-over-month regression gates.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — read-only.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers metric-delta surfacing and exit 6 on regression.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop cmd_compare and the parser entry.

entries.evidence: Round-fifteen improvement #4 — compare_grade_distribution reported counts/ratios but not per-grade PSI contribution.
entries.root_cause: PSI was only available via the dedicated metric_psi module.
entries.targeted_fix: Add psi_contribution column to compare_grade_distribution using (cur - base) * log(cur / base) with an epsilon guard.
entries.expected_benefit: Per-grade PSI contribution is now available alongside count/ratio comparison.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive column; existing column set unchanged.
entries.validation_method: pytest verifies the column exists and total contribution is positive and finite.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the psi_contribution computation.

entries.evidence: Round-fifteen improvement #5 — PSI bin count was hard-coded to 10 with no override.
entries.root_cause: calculate_psi(bins=10) was inlined.
entries.targeted_fix: Add --psi-bins (int, default 10) to cmd_validate; passes through to metric_psi.calculate_psi.
entries.expected_benefit: Operators can tune bin count without code changes (e.g., 20 for fine-grained PSI).
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — default preserves prior behavior.
entries.validation_method: Existing PSI tests still pass; CLI surface tested via docs-cli drift baseline.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore hard-coded bins=10.

entries.evidence: Round-fifteen improvement #6 — policy entries could remain in 'proposed' indefinitely without warning.
entries.root_cause: No invariant ensured 'proposed' progressed to 'applied' or 'validated'.
entries.targeted_fix: Add opt-in test gated by QVA_STRICT_PROPOSED=1 that fails when any threshold_policy.json entry is still in the 'proposed' state.
entries.expected_benefit: Operations can flip the env var once the workflow guarantees timely transitions.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — opt-in only.
entries.validation_method: Default suite skips it; manual runs with QVA_STRICT_PROPOSED=1 verify the assertion.
entries.rollback_rule: Drop the test.

entries.evidence: Round-sixteen improvement — check_rank_ordering's n_inversions formula was convoluted and direction-agnostic.
entries.root_cause: The original expression embedded a conditional triple-nested expression with unclear semantics for mixed-direction series.
entries.targeted_fix: Replace n_inversions with explicit n_strict_increases, n_strict_decreases, plus a direction-aware n_violations computed against the caller-supplied `expected` (ascending/descending). Reject other values with ValueError.
entries.expected_benefit: Clear, auditable rank-ordering output; callers no longer have to interpret a single overloaded counter.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — no other callsite referenced the removed n_inversions field.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers the monotonic happy path, descending-expected violation counts, and bad-expected ValueError.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore the previous return-dict shape.

entries.evidence: User asked for the latest Basel / Korean FSS alignment. CLAUDE.md §2 forbids regulatory interpretation, so a structural capability-to-area map is the appropriate response.
entries.root_cause: Validation tools had no documented mapping to widely known supervisory framework areas; reviewers could not easily see which tool covers which concern.
entries.targeted_fix: Add docs/regulatory_framework_mapping.md with internal area codes (basel_irb_pd / basel_irb_lgd / basel_irb_ead / basel_stress / basel_moc / ifrs9_pd / mrm_basel / fss_bank_validation / fss_credit_scoring / governance) and explicit disclaimers that the document is not a regulatory citation.
entries.expected_benefit: Reviewers map outputs to their compliance review without ambiguity; agent stays inside CLAUDE.md §2 by not citing specific paragraphs.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — documentation only.
entries.validation_method: Manual review.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the file.

entries.evidence: PD backtesting was missing a Basel-style binomial traffic-light helper that reviewers expect.
entries.root_cause: calibration_test.py covered HL/Spiegelhalter/binomial-per-bucket but no zone-based aggregator.
entries.targeted_fix: Add traffic_light_per_bucket + classify_zone + aggregate_zone implementing the standard one-sided binomial p-value zoning (Green: p >= alpha_green, Yellow: alpha_red <= p < alpha_green, Red: p < alpha_red). Defaults alpha_green=0.05, alpha_red=0.0001.
entries.expected_benefit: PD backtests can produce a familiar red/yellow/green view without writing custom code.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive module; scipy.binom with manual fallback.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers calibrated (Green/Yellow), severely under-predicted (Red), per-bucket aggregation, p=0 corner, and bad-alpha rejection.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the module + test.

entries.evidence: MoC is a widely used IRB add-on concept; aggregating components by category A/B/C was being done ad hoc.
entries.root_cause: No reusable helper existed.
entries.targeted_fix: Add build_moc_table + aggregate_moc + apply_moc. Validates non-negative values, rejects unknown categories, and emits a structured by_category breakdown plus a total_moc.
entries.expected_benefit: Consistent reporting of A/B/C decomposition and additive add-on application across runs.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive helper.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers happy path, empty, invalid category, negative value, missing fields.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the module + test.

entries.evidence: LGD validation lacked an explicit downturn identification + comparison helper.
entries.root_cause: summarize_error_by_segment only handled categorical splits; downturn periods required period-indicator joins.
entries.targeted_fix: Add identify_downturn_periods (direction-aware threshold) and compute_downturn_lgd (joins observations to flags, reports downturn vs non-downturn means + diff + unmatched-period count).
entries.expected_benefit: Downturn LGD reporting becomes a single pair of calls; caller still supplies the indicator and threshold per policy.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — pure additive helper.
entries.validation_method: pytest covers higher_is_worse / lower_is_worse direction, bad-direction error, mean computation, unmatched-period accounting.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete the module + test.

entries.component: harness/threshold_policy.schema.json + harness/threshold_policy.json + tools/threshold_loader.py + quant_validation_agent/cli.py + tests/test_threshold_loader.py + tests/test_cli.py
entries.evidence: Reviewers needed each threshold to declare its regulatory area without inviting paragraph-level citation.
entries.root_cause: Policy entries had no regulatory metadata.
entries.targeted_fix: Add optional regulatory_basis (array of strings) to metricDefinition in the schema. Tag KS/AUROC/AR/PSI/Brier/MAE_LGD with internal area codes in the live policy. Loader surfaces the list; _rag_with_threshold passes it through; every validate metric block now carries regulatory_basis.

entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — additive field; current loader callers ignore the new key.

entries.root_cause: Existing templates focused per-model only.
entries.targeted_fix: Add a request template that lists internal area codes, references the policy file's regulatory_basis tags, and shows runnable CLI / Python snippets for PD discrimination, calibration, backtest traffic light, LGD downturn, and MoC aggregation.
entries.expected_benefit: Newcomers can scope a regulator-aligned validation run end-to-end without ad-hoc scripts.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — documentation only.

entries.evidence: Round 17 added no new CLI flags but did widen the regulatory_basis surface in every metric block. Re-pin the docs-cli baseline after the manifest run.
entries.root_cause: The committed baseline file is enforced by tests/test_docs_cli_drift.py.
entries.targeted_fix: Regenerate docs/cli_reference.md via `python -m quant_validation_agent docs-cli` and commit the result.
entries.expected_benefit: Drift test stays green.
entries.expected_regression_risk: None — baseline only.
entries.validation_method: pytest tests/test_docs_cli_drift.py.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore the previous baseline.

entries.evidence: Sector accuracy testing exposed that build_lift_table divided by base_rate twice. Standard cumulative lift = cum_default_share / cum_pop_share. The bug inflated lift by 1/base_rate; e.g., a random model showed lift ≈ 1/base_rate instead of 1.0 across deciles, and cumulative lift at the bottom approached 1/base_rate instead of 1.0.
entries.root_cause: Implementation included an extra '/ max(base_rate, 1e-12)' factor; the shares already normalize by totals.
entries.targeted_fix: Drop the extra divisor. Cumulative lift now = cum_default_share / cum_pop_share. Standard properties restored: random scoring yields lift ≈ 1.0; perfect separator's top decile lift = min(1/base_rate, 10); bottom row lift exactly 1.0.
entries.expected_benefit: lift_top_decile RAG thresholds (G:2.0/Y:1.5) now have the standard practitioner interpretation.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Medium — every prior run that emitted lift_top_decile values divided them by base_rate; new numbers are 1/base_rate smaller and may flip RAG. Recompute against current data before relying on the threshold values.
entries.validation_method: Sector accuracy tests in tests/sectors/test_decile_lift_accuracy.py verify standard properties. Full pytest run: 368 passed, 3 opt-in skipped.
entries.rollback_rule: Restore the extra '/ max(base_rate, 1e-12)' divisor in build_lift_table.

entries.component: tests/sectors/ (11 new modules, 58 new tests) + tests/sectors/fixtures.py
entries.evidence: User requested diverse sector-level sample data and accuracy verification.
entries.root_cause: Existing tests confirmed code-runs-without-error and basic shape, but did not verify metric values against analytical / closed-form expectations.
entries.targeted_fix: Add tests/sectors/fixtures.py (deterministic generators with seeds) and 11 sector test modules: discrimination (AUC against Phi(shift/sqrt(2))), calibration (Brier against E[p(1-p)], HL/Spiegelhalter rejection vs non-rejection), PSI (identical/shift/disjoint), LGD/EAD (constant-error closed forms), CDR/SDR (arithmetic), scenario (monotonic vs engineered violation), regression (collinear vs orthogonal VIF/CI), stationarity (white noise vs random walk ADF), traffic light (calibrated/severe/borderline binomial zones), decile lift (random/perfect-separator standard formulas), downturn LGD (mean diff arithmetic).
entries.expected_benefit: Accuracy-grade coverage that surfaces numerical bugs (caught the decile_lift bug above) and pins expectations for every tool.
entries.expected_regression_risk: Low — purely additive test modules with deterministic seeds.
entries.validation_method: All 58 sector tests pass. Total suite: 368 passed, 3 opt-in skipped.
entries.rollback_rule: Delete tests/sectors/ tree.
